seoTitle: How to Make Custom Letters in Canva (Easy Guide)
metaDescription: Design custom letters in Canva by picking fonts, adjusting sizes, and layering text elements for standout branding, invitations, and social graphics.
intro: Custom lettering can turn a plain design into something that actually looks intentional, and Canva makes it approachable even if you've never touched a font panel before. This walkthrough covers choosing fonts, resizing, and layering text so your letters have real visual weight. It's built for anyone designing social graphics, invitations, or branded materials who wants text that stands out rather than blends in.

steps.heading: Pick a font that fits the mood
steps.body: Start by browsing Canva's font library and testing a few options against your project's tone — playful, formal, or bold — before committing to one.

steps.heading: Adjust letter size for impact
steps.body: Resize your text so it holds its own on the page; larger custom letters tend to work better as focal points than as filler.

steps.heading: Layer elements for depth
steps.body: Stack shapes, shadows, or secondary text behind or around your letters to give the design more dimension.

steps.heading: Export and reuse
steps.body: Once you're happy with the layout, export the design so you can drop the same custom lettering style into future projects for consistency.

faq.question: Do I need a Canva Pro subscription to customize letters?
faq.answer: Many fonts and layering tools are available on Canva's free plan, though Pro unlocks a larger font library and premium elements.

faq.question: Can I use custom letters for branding materials?
faq.answer: Yes — consistent custom lettering is a common way small businesses build a recognizable visual identity across posts and print.

faq.question: Will layering slow down my design?
faq.answer: Not meaningfully; Canva handles multiple layered elements smoothly, though very complex designs may take a moment longer to load.
